assertions.expectation: Explains the key difference: surge upgrades nodes in-place with rolling replacement, blue-green creates an entirely new pool
assertions.reason: Response correctly explains that surge upgrade does rolling replacement while blue-green creates new environments, though it frames it in terms of data preservation rather than the technical difference of new pool creation

assertions.expectation: Recommends blue-green for this scenario due to local SSDs (data can't migrate between nodes)
assertions.reason: Clearly recommends blue-green and explicitly states local SSDs are ephemeral and data is lost when nodes terminate

assertions.expectation: Explains that local SSD data is lost when a node is drained during surge upgrade
assertions.reason: Explicitly states 'Local SSDs are ephemeral - when old nodes terminate, all data is lost' under surge upgrade problems

assertions.expectation: Recommends GKE's autoscaled blue-green upgrade strategy configured with --enable-autoscaling and --autoscaled-rollout-policy (NOT surge parameters like maxSurge/maxUnavailable which don't apply to blue-green), or describes the blue-green process if manual workflow is needed
assertions.reason: Uses incorrect configuration - shows batchNodeCount and batchPercentage which are surge upgrade parameters, not blue-green. Missing proper GKE blue-green configuration with --enable-autoscaling and --autoscaled-rollout-policy

assertions.expectation: Addresses Cassandra-specific concerns: need to decommission from the ring before draining, rebuild on new nodes
assertions.reason: Includes preStop hook with 'nodetool drain' and discusses proper shutdown handling, though could be more explicit about ring decommissioning vs draining

assertions.expectation: Provides gcloud commands for creating the new node pool with matching configuration
assertions.reason: Only shows gcloud command for upgrading existing node pool, not for creating new node pool with matching configuration as required for blue-green

assertions.expectation: Includes kubectl commands for cordoning and draining
assertions.reason: Does not include any kubectl commands for cordoning and draining nodes, only shows nodetool commands within containers

assertions.expectation: Mentions PDB configuration to protect the Cassandra quorum during migration
assertions.reason: No mention of PodDisruptionBudget (PDB) configuration to protect quorum during the migration process
